items.condition: Sustained regression in permitted operating domains
items.detail: If major jurisdictions roll back prior approvals without replacement pathways, the urban-first scaling assumption is likely wrong.

items.condition: ADS safety performance consistently worse than credible human baselines
items.detail: If transparent, comparable data shows persistent inferiority on frequency or severity where the thesis assumed convergence, liability and adoption timelines extend materially.

items.condition: Insurer results improve structurally without AV-related frequency explanation
items.detail: If personal-auto combined ratios tighten durably while autonomous miles rise, the short thesis on legacy auto risk pools may be mis-specified.

items.condition: OEM and supplier capex reallocates entirely to L2/L3 with L4 city programs shelved
items.detail: If capital and talent exit urban autonomy for assist-only roadmaps, enabler multiples may compress and fleet-led outcomes dominate—hurting broad OEM transition longs.

items.condition: Utilization and pricing collapse in ride-hail without AV cost offsets
items.detail: If fleet economics deteriorate due to oversupply or regulation before autonomy lowers marginal cost, the ownership-to-utilization shift slows.

items.condition: Residual values for passenger vehicles strengthen broadly for multiple years
items.detail: If ownership demand remains robust alongside AV pilots, the utilization and depreciation channel of the thesis weakens.
